description: External challenge feed for Clarvis evolution loop. Challenges from benchmarks, research papers, and coding tasks that test novel problem-solving beyond self-generated work.

challenges.title: Multi-step logical deduction: solve a constraint satisfaction puzzle
challenges.description: Implement a solver for a 5-variable constraint satisfaction problem (CSP) using backtracking with arc consistency (AC-3). Create 3 test puzzles of increasing difficulty (easy: 3 constraints, medium: 7, hard: 12). Measure: nodes explored, backtracks, solve time. Compare against brute-force baseline.

challenges.title: Implement analogical reasoning between brain memories
challenges.description: Build an analogy engine: given a source pair (A:B), find the best matching target pair (C:D) from brain memories. Use embedding offsets (B-A ≈ D-C) to detect structural analogies. Test on 10 analogy queries against ClarvisDB. Report analogy accuracy and whether the engine discovers non-obvious connections.

challenges.title: Implement Socratic self-questioning for reasoning chains
challenges.description: Add a Socratic questioning layer to reasoning_chains.py: after each reasoning step, auto-generate one devil's advocate question ('But what if...?', 'How do we know...?'). If the question reveals a weakness, add a correction step. Test on 5 recent reasoning chains: measure whether Socratic questioning catches logical gaps.

challenges.title: Build a causal inference engine for episode outcome analysis
challenges.description: Given episode data (task, actions, outcome), build a lightweight causal model: which actions most influence success/failure? Use counterfactual analysis (if action X hadn't happened, would outcome change?). Apply to last 50 episodes. Report top 5 causal factors for success and failure.

challenges.title: Implement argument mapping for wiki claims
challenges.description: Build an argument mapper: given a wiki page with Key Claims, extract the argument structure (premises → conclusion, supports/rebuts relations). Output a directed graph of arguments. Visualize as ASCII art. Test on 3 wiki pages. Identify unsupported claims (conclusions without premises).

challenges.title: Cross-domain knowledge synthesis: find unexpected connections
challenges.description: Build a synthesis engine: take 2 random wiki pages from different domains, compute their embedding centroid, search the brain for memories near that centroid, and write a synthesis note connecting the domains. Test 5 random pairs. Score each synthesis on novelty (not obvious from either page alone) and coherence.

challenges.title: Implement a persistent skip list with O(log n) search
challenges.description: Build a persistent (immutable) skip list data structure supporting insert, delete, and search in O(log n). Each modification returns a new version while sharing structure with previous versions. Include a version history that allows querying any past state. Benchmark against Python's sorted containers.

challenges.title: Build a minimal theorem prover for propositional logic
challenges.description: Implement a resolution-based theorem prover for propositional logic: parse formulas (AND, OR, NOT, IMPLIES), convert to CNF, apply resolution rule until proven or saturated. Support: modus ponens, contradiction detection, proof trace output. Test with 10 theorems from classical logic.

challenges.title: Implement Levin search for small program synthesis
challenges.description: Build a Levin universal search: enumerate programs in a minimal language (arithmetic + conditionals + loops), ordered by length, with runtime budget proportional to 2^(-length). Synthesize programs for 5 input-output specifications (e.g., double, factorial, fibonacci). Measure: programs explored, time to solution.

challenges.title: Build a calibration curve plotter from prediction history
challenges.description: Read data/calibration/predictions.jsonl, bin predictions by confidence (0.5-0.6, 0.6-0.7, ..., 0.9-1.0), compute actual success rate per bin. Plot as ASCII calibration curve. Compute ECE (Expected Calibration Error). Identify the most miscalibrated bin and suggest a correction factor.

challenges.title: Implement a memory consolidation quality test
challenges.description: Test whether brain.optimize_full() actually improves retrieval quality. Methodology: (1) baseline retrieval on 20 gold queries, (2) run optimize_full, (3) re-test same queries. Measure: nDCG change, latency change, memory count change. Report whether consolidation helps or hurts.

challenges.title: Implement a regex engine from scratch (Thompson NFA)
challenges.description: Build a regex engine using Thompson's NFA construction: support concatenation, alternation (|), Kleene star (*), plus (+), optional (?), and character classes [a-z]. Convert regex to NFA, simulate NFA for matching. Test against 20 patterns with known match/no-match inputs. Compare correctness against Python re module.

challenges.title: Implement a confidence calibration feedback loop
challenges.description: Build a feedback loop: after each episode, compare predicted confidence vs actual outcome. Maintain a rolling accuracy-by-confidence-band table. When a band is consistently overconfident (predicted 0.9 but actual 0.7), auto-adjust future predictions downward. Integrate with clarvis_confidence.py. Test with historical data.

challenges.title: Implement contradiction detection across wiki pages
challenges.description: Build a contradiction detector: for each pair of wiki pages that share a tag, compare their Key Claims via embedding similarity. Flag pairs where claims are semantically similar but contain negation or opposing terms. Test across all current wiki pages. Report found contradictions with evidence.

challenges.title: Implement A* search with multiple heuristics on a graph puzzle
challenges.description: Build A* search for the 15-puzzle (4x4 sliding tile puzzle). Implement 3 heuristics: Manhattan distance, linear conflict, and pattern database (3x3 corner). Compare: nodes expanded, solution length, time. Demonstrate that better heuristics dramatically reduce search space. Solve 5 random instances.

challenges.title: Implement BEIR-style retrieval benchmark for ClarvisDB
challenges.description: Create a retrieval benchmark using 50+ query-document pairs with known relevance labels. Measure nDCG@10, MAP, and Recall@k. Compare ClarvisDB's MiniLM embeddings against BM25 baseline on the same queries. Output a benchmark report to data/benchmarks/.

challenges.title: Solve 5 ARC-AGI-style pattern completion puzzles programmatically
challenges.description: Implement a solver for grid-based pattern completion (ARC-AGI style). Create 5 test puzzles with known solutions. The solver should use rule extraction (symmetry, rotation, color mapping) without LLM calls. Measure solve rate and time.

challenges.title: Write a property-based test suite for ClarvisDB graph operations
challenges.description: Use Hypothesis library to generate random graph operations (add_edge, remove_edge, traverse) and verify invariants: no orphan edges after cleanup, bidirectional consistency, cycle detection correctness. Target: 100+ test cases auto-generated.

challenges.title: Implement Sparse Priming Representations (SPR) for brain memory compression
challenges.description: From the SPR paper: compress long memories into minimal token priming representations that reconstruct meaning when fed to an LLM. Implement encode_spr() and decode_spr() functions. Test on 20 existing brain memories: measure compression ratio and semantic preservation (cosine similarity of embeddings before/after).

challenges.title: Implement a simple Transformer attention visualization for brain search
challenges.description: Build a lightweight attention-weight visualizer that shows which query tokens most influence retrieval results. Use the existing MiniLM embeddings to compute token-level attention scores. Output an HTML report showing query-document attention heatmaps for the last 10 brain searches.

challenges.title: Implement a lock-free concurrent ring buffer in Python
challenges.description: Build a thread-safe ring buffer using only atomics (no threading.Lock). Support multiple producers and single consumer. Benchmark throughput vs the current Lock-based approach. Include stress tests with 8 producer threads.

challenges.title: Build a minimal DAG task scheduler with dependency resolution
challenges.description: Implement a task scheduler that takes a DAG of tasks with dependencies, topologically sorts them, and executes independent tasks in parallel using asyncio. Include cycle detection, timeout handling, and a dry-run mode. Write tests covering diamond dependencies, fan-out/fan-in, and error propagation.

challenges.title: Implement incremental TF-IDF for streaming document indexing
challenges.description: Build an incremental TF-IDF index that can add documents one at a time without recomputing the entire corpus. Support search queries returning top-k results. Compare accuracy against sklearn's TfidfVectorizer on 100 brain memories. Target: <5% accuracy loss vs batch TF-IDF.

challenges.title: Chaos test: verify brain recovery after simulated corruption
challenges.description: Create a chaos testing harness that: (1) backs up current brain state, (2) corrupts 10% of ChromaDB metadata randomly, (3) runs brain.health_check() and brain.optimize-full, (4) verifies recovery by checking recall accuracy on golden QA set, (5) restores backup. Report which corruption types are recoverable and which are not.

challenges.title: Profile and optimize the 3 slowest brain operations end-to-end
challenges.description: Use cProfile to profile the top 3 slowest brain operations (likely: store with graph update, multi-collection search, batch decay). For each, create a flamegraph-style report, identify the bottleneck, and implement one optimization. Measure before/after latency. Target: 20%+ improvement on at least one operation.

challenges.title: Implement a simple MemoryBank with forgetting curve (Ebbinghaus)
challenges.description: Build a memory bank that models Ebbinghaus forgetting curves. Each memory has a retention strength that decays exponentially. Implement spaced repetition: memories that are recalled get strength boosts at increasing intervals. Compare with ClarvisDB's current linear decay. Test on 50 memories over simulated 30-day period.

challenges.title: Build a git-diff semantic analyzer that classifies changes
challenges.description: Create a tool that reads a git diff and classifies each hunk as: bugfix, feature, refactor, test, docs, or config. Use heuristics (file paths, changed line patterns, commit message) — no LLM calls. Test against the last 20 commits in this repo and report classification accuracy vs manual labels.

challenges.title: Measure context window utilization efficiency across 10 heartbeats
challenges.description: Instrument the context brief generation to measure: (a) total tokens generated, (b) tokens actually relevant to the selected task (via post-hoc analysis), (c) wasted tokens (irrelevant sections). Report utilization ratio. Target insight: identify the top 3 sources of context waste.

challenges.title: Implement a bloom filter for fast duplicate detection in brain.store()
challenges.description: Add a Bloom filter as a fast pre-check before the expensive ChromaDB cosine similarity dedup in brain.store(). Tune false positive rate to <1%. Measure: (a) how many expensive dedup calls are avoided, (b) store() latency improvement. Include serialization so the filter persists across restarts.

challenges.title: Implement chain-of-thought self-evaluation for episode quality scoring
challenges.description: Build an evaluator that scores episode quality by analyzing the reasoning chain: (1) count reasoning steps, (2) check for backtracking/correction, (3) measure conclusion support (does the output follow from the chain?), (4) detect shallow reasoning patterns. Score 0-1. Validate against 20 recent episodes with manual quality labels.
